title: Drain Grates
desc: The pit drains under the arena sand need clearing before tonight's bouts turn the floor into something sloppy.

steps.text:
The Colosseum sand is meant to hold a fight, not preserve it. Under the surface, a ring of drain grates and wash troughs keeps blood, rinse water, and everything else moving away from the center before the next bout begins. Most spectators never think about that system, which is the highest compliment an arena drain can receive. Today the western run is slow enough that the pit master has started thinking about it angrily.

He hands you a grate hook and a flat shovel. "Lift and clear the drain mouths if you want the unpleasant truth of the matter," he says. "Or pull fresh sand over the grates and reset the surface grade once I open them. Both jobs keep tonight from becoming muddy for the wrong reasons."

options.label: Lift the grates and clear the drain mouths below
Reset the sand grade over the reopened drain line

steps.text:
The western grates are heavier than they look and fouler than anyone would willingly describe before breakfast. You hook each one up in turn, shovel out the thick packed mix beneath, and flush the lower throat until the trapped sludge breaks free and the water in the test bucket vanishes with the speed the pit master wanted in the first place. It is brutal work and entirely necessary.

By the last grate, the smell has improved only by comparison, but the drain line is hungry again. When the pit master splashes a second bucket across the trench, it disappears cleanly under the sand instead of pooling along the edge. He seems almost cheerful for half a second. "Good. Let the arena keep its dignity a little longer."

options.label: Drop the grates back in place and leave the line drawing well

steps.text:
Once the western mouths are clear, the sand has to be laid back over them with enough slope to shed fluid and enough cover to keep a fighter's footing honest. You pull the fresh red sand across the grate line in measured passes, smoothing the surface until the pit floor reads as one continuous field rather than a set of hidden mechanical intentions. The pit master checks the grade by spilling a narrow stream of rinse water at the outer ring and watching where it travels.

The water runs exactly where it should: toward the drains, under the surface, out of sight before it can insult the footing. That is all the proof he needs. He drives the smoothing board into the sand one last time and steps back. "There. A proper arena floor. Let tonight's violence look effortless."

options.label: Set the shovel aside and leave the pit floor cleanly graded
